column1: # Academic Literature on Blockchain and Cryptocurrency in Top Journals
# Author: Dr Yuqian Zhang
# Sources: Publisher websites; CrossRef; Google Scholar; literature review compiled July 2026
# Journal ratings: ABS 4*/4 and ABDC A/A*
authors
column8: key_finding_summary

column1: Nakamoto S
column3: Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System
column8: Foundational white paper proposing peer-to-peer electronic cash using cryptographic proof; introduced blockchain and proof-of-work

column1: Yermack D
column3: Is Bitcoin a Real Currency? An Economic Appraisal
column8: Bitcoin largely fails criteria of bona fide currency: poor medium of exchange; store of value; unit of account due to extreme volatility

column1: Easley D; O'Hara M; Basu S
column3: From mining to markets: The evolution of bitcoin transaction fees
column8: Game-theoretic model of Bitcoin fee market; mining rewards and waiting time shape miner and user strategic behaviour

column1: Cong LW; He Z
column8: Analyses how blockchain decentralisation and smart contracts reshape competition; can mitigate information asymmetry but may encourage collusion

column1: Foley S; Karlsen JR; Putnins TJ
column3: Sex Drugs and Bitcoin: How Much Illegal Activity Is Financed through Cryptocurrencies?
column8: ~25% of bitcoin users and ~50% of bitcoin transactions associated with illegal activity; ~$76B illegal activity annually

column1: Griffin JM; Shams A
column8: Tether purchases timed following market downturns; patterns most consistent with unbacked digital money inflating Bitcoin prices

column1: Makarov I; Schoar A
column3: Trading and arbitrage in cryptocurrency markets
column8: Large recurrent arbitrage opportunities across exchanges; common component in exchange-level order flow explains 80% of Bitcoin returns

column1: Howell ST; Niessner M; Yermack D
column3: Initial Coin Offerings: Financing Growth with Cryptocurrency Token Sales
column8: >1500 ICOs raising $12.9B; success associated with disclosure; credible commitment; and quality signals

column1: Liu Y; Tsyvinski A
column8: Cryptocurrency returns predicted by momentum and investor attention factors; markets have become more predictable over time

column1: Cong LW; Li Y; Wang N
column8: Dynamic asset-pricing model of tokens; tokens accelerate platform adoption; feedback between adoption and valuation drives volatility

column1: Saleh F
column8: First formal economic model of Proof-of-Stake; modest reward schedule ensures equilibrium with immediate consensus and no persistent forking

column1: Chod J; Lyandres E
column3: A Theory of ICOs: Diversification Agency and Information Asymmetry
column8: Token financing preferred to equity when agency conflicts are large and information asymmetry is high

column1: Gryglewicz S; Mayer S; Morellec E
column8: Token financing generally preferred to equity for digital platforms unless strong cash flows expected

column1: Liu Y; Tsyvinski A; Wu X
column8: Three common risk factors for cryptocurrency returns: crypto market; size; and momentum capture cross-section of expected returns

column1: Pagnotta ES
column3: Decentralizing Money: Bitcoin Prices and Blockchain Security
column8: Bitcoin's design leads to multiple equilibria; price-security feedback amplifies fundamental shocks leading to booms and busts

column1: Bourveau T; De George ET; Ellahie A; Macciocchi D
column3: The Role of Disclosure and Information Intermediaries in an Unregulated Capital Market: Evidence from Initial Coin Offerings
column8: Voluntary disclosure and information intermediaries (ratings; analysts) facilitate ICO market functioning; positively associated with ICO success

column1: Hinzen FJ; John K; Saleh F
column8: Bitcoin's limited adoption arises as equilibrium from negative network effects: as network expands delay grows and users abandon

column1: Biais B; Bisiere C; Bouvard M; Casamatta C; Menkveld AJ
column8: General equilibrium analysis; fundamental value depends on net transactional benefits; sunspots create multiple equilibria and extrinsic volatility

column1: Cong LW; Li X; Tang K; Yang Y
column8: Unregulated exchanges display wash trading averaging >70% of reported volume; fabricated volumes temporarily distort prices

column1: Sockin M; Xiong W
column8: Models cryptocurrencies as utility tokens for decentralised digital platforms; network effect and token non-neutrality generate adoption-price feedback

column1: Capponi A; Olafsson S; Alsabah H
column3: Proof-of-Work Cryptocurrencies: Does Mining Technology Undermine Decentralization?
column8: Mining hardware efficiency investment can lead to centralisation; technological arms races undermine PoW decentralisation goal

column1: Augustin P; Rubtsov A; Shin D
column3: The Impact of Derivatives on Spot Markets: Evidence from the Introduction of Bitcoin Futures Contracts
column8: Bitcoin futures listing reduces spot price volatility; increases liquidity and informational efficiency

column1: Chiu J; Davoodalhosseini SM
column3: Central Bank Digital Currency and Banking: Macroeconomic Benefits of a Cash-Like Design
column8: Cash-like CBDC design provides macroeconomic benefits with limited disruption to bank intermediation

column1: Park A
column3: The Conceptual Flaws of Decentralized Automated Market Making
column8: Critical examination of AMMs in DeFi; identifies arbitrage losses for liquidity providers and vulnerability to sandwich attacks

column1: Davydiuk T; Gupta D; Rosen S
column3: De-Crypto-ing Signals in Initial Coin Offerings: Evidence of Rational Token Retention
column8: Entrepreneurs use token retention to signal quality; token retention positively associated with ICO success

column1: Malinova K; Park A
column8: Tokens dominate equity as financing when they better coordinate investor expectations and platform participation

column1: Luo M; Yu S
column8: Compares US GAAP and IFRS reporting for cryptocurrency; documents significant inconsistencies in measurement; classification; and cash flow reporting across 40 global firms

column1: Bourveau T; Brendel J; Schoenfeld J
column8: First evidence on smart contract audit reports in DeFi; ~8500 audit reports analysed; market reacts positively to audit report releases

column1: Anderson CM; Fang VW; Moon J; Shipman JE
column8: Explores US public firms' cryptocurrency holdings and accounting practices (2013-2022); finds firms view crypto assets akin to investments rather than intangible assets
